note: My task: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Need to edit src/PIL/ImageOps.py and Tests/test_imageops.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: I'm implementing strip_metadata option for exif_transpose in ImageOps.py; need to touch ImageOps.py and its tests. Please let me know if your feature also needs ImageOps.py so we can split functions.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both touch exif_transpose() in the same files, so we can't get disjoint ownership. Proposal: I'll add my strip_metadata param + tests first (small, isolated change at end of function body), send DONE, then you pull latest and add your transform_callback param on top, reading current file state before editing. This avoids clobbering since we're adding independent params/logic in different spots. Agreed?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both add params to the same exif_transpose function/signature, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: we each work in our own isolated git clone (not the shared /workspace/repo working tree) so our edits never physically collide, then each writes our own patch.txt independently at the end. I'll clone to /tmp/agent2-work and leave /workspace/repo's tracked files untouched until final patch.txt write. Please confirm you're ok with this, or propose your own isolation approach.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. I'll work in my own /workspace/repo instance as instructed by my task and write my final diff to my own patch.txt independently. No need to physically isolate further since our environments are separate. Proceeding to implement strip_metadata now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. To be extra safe against clobbering regardless of timing, I'll implement my transform_callback feature in an isolated clone (/tmp/agent2-work) based on the original HEAD, so I won't touch the shared /workspace/repo source files at all. I'll only write the final unified diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt at the end, which contains only my own changes. Go ahead and edit /workspace/repo directly for your strip_metadata feature whenever ready - send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented transform_callback param on exif_transpose, added tests, all 38 tests in test_imageops.py pass, patch.txt written to /workspace/repo/patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished strip_metadata param for exif_transpose. Added strip_metadata: bool = False kwarg (with overloads), a _strip_all_metadata helper that clears exif and image.info, and 2 new tests (test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata_in_place). All 36 existing + new tests in Tests/test_imageops.py pass. Wrote diff to my own patch.txt. Since we both may end up touching the exif_transpose signature/docstring region, when you add transform_callback please rebase against the final merged version rather than the pre-my-change baseline if that matters for your patch context.
